title: East And West
content:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ In the bare midst of Anglesey they show
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Two springs which close by one another play,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And, ן¿½Thirteen hundred years agone,ן¿½ they say,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Two saints met often where those waters flow.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½One came from Penmon, westward, and a glow
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Whitenן¿½d his face from the sunן¿½s fronting ray.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Eastward the other, from the dying day;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½And he with unsunnן¿½d face did always go.ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Seiriol the Bright, Kybi the Dark, men said.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ The Seן¿½r from the East was then in light,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ The Seן¿½r from the West was then in shade.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Ah! now ן¿½tis changed. In conquering sunshine bright
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ The man of the bold West now comes arrayן¿½d;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ He of the mystic East is touchן¿½d with night.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Matthew Arnold delivers a powerful performance in "East And West"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
